generatedSentence: A nascent newbie, I tried to notice the lowbrow style of materialists chewing ungodly popcorn, a musical prelude to my egocentric uncle's rage. He'd often rampage, making us kowtow, despite his supposed finesse with keyboards. The news, a bubble of avocado and pickles, was sending an urgent message, but all I saw was dust and the promise of a lobster trophy. An interesting definition of 'winner' came from faraway Nan, whose demented wacko of an uncle owned a property trailer, always awaking to the doorbell's noises. He'd grill generously, yet his mercurial temperament could hinge on an onion, turning a slacker's joke into a juggernaut of jams; I struggled to understand this volatile individual, a veritable grenade of personality. A ridiculous knight, I joked about my motivation while linking notebooks, but the workplace heights were unreal; a slapper kicked my wakeboard, a move investigators might review as an awesome plot to kill. Stars became a blur as I prepared to upload the plank, realizing my friendship with the viper-like dolls was just bad luck. To involve and destroy such unique mankind was overkill, a lack of understanding that could lead to drowning in the lowdown of the badlands. Kent and my uncles, once trump, would outjump any brick wall, swiftly implementing acupuncture, lest their neglect of the pool lead to a krone's flyway. A tasty whistle might represent our bohemian resolve, ensuring no one kills our joy.

He'd grill generously, yet his mercurial temperament could hinge on an onion, turning a slacker's joke into a juggernaut of jams; I struggled to understand this volatile individual, a veritable grenade of personality.

A ridiculous knight, I joked about my motivation while linking notebooks, but the workplace heights were unreal; a slapper kicked my wakeboard, a move investigators might review as an awesome plot to kill. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A protagonist with a shimmering silver helmet and glowing emerald eyes, wearing a tattered, dark purple cape. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist standing in a dusty, dimly lit arcade, observing a group of figures intensely focused on retro keyboards, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist's demented uncle, with an onion on his head, furiously grilling at a roadside trailer, close-up on his volatile expression. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, dressed as a knight, being kicked off a wakeboard mid-air over a city skyline, dynamic action shot from below. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist and several uncles performing acupuncture on a brick pool, with a unique krone bird flying overhead, low-angle shot.

definitions.definition: Tropæum.
An object, usually in the form of a statuette, cup, or shield, awarded for success in a competition or to mark a special achievement.
definitions.example: He won the trophy in a running competition.

definitions.definition: An object taken as a prize by a hunter or conqueror, especially one that is displayed.
definitions.example: The set of antlers which hung on the wall was his prized trophy.

definitions.definition: Any emblem of success; a status symbol.
definitions.example: His trophies included his second wife, his successful children, the third and fourth homes in Palm Beach and Malibu, his three yachts (for the Pacific, the Atlantic, and the Mediterranean), his jet, and his mistresses.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An object taken by a serial killer or rapist as a memento of the crime.

A display of weaponry and other militaria, often captured from a defeated enemy, as an ornament designed for the purpose of triumphalist display by a victor or as a show of military prowess by a monarch.
(etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
A component of many instruments including the piano, organ, and harpsichord consisting of usually black and white keys that cause different tones to be produced when struck.
A device with keys of a musical keyboard, used to control electronic sound-producing devices which may be built into or separate from the keyboard device.
To type on a computer keyboard.
definitions.example: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.

definitions.definition: Of a person: lacking reverence for God; of an action: not in accordance with God's will or religious teachings.
Immoral, sinful, or wicked.
definitions.example: People often turn to ungodly ideas as an emotional response to trauma.

definitions.definition: Extreme; unreasonable.
definitions.example: We had to start our journey at an ungodly hour.

definitions.definition: The brother or brother-in-law of one’s parent.
The male cousin of one’s parent.
A companion to one's (usually unmarried) mother.
A source of advice, encouragement, or help.
A pawnbroker.
An affectionate term for a man of an older generation than oneself, especially a friend of one's parents, by means of fictive kin.
An older male African-American person.
To address somebody by the term uncle.
To act like, or as, an uncle.
A cry used to indicate surrender.
A snack food made from corn/maize kernels popped by dry heating.
A type of maize with a hard outer hull that, along with the type of starch it contains, makes it suitable for popping.
A kind of stitch similar to a bobble.
(by extension) Entertainment.
(Of a guinea pig) to stand up or jump up quickly.
Someone who is materialistic, concerned only with material possessions.
A follower or proponent of philosophical materialism.
To crush with the teeth by repeated closing and opening of the jaws; done to food to soften it and break it down by the action of saliva before it is swallowed.
definitions.example: Make sure to chew thoroughly, and don't talk with your mouth full!

definitions.definition: To grind, tear, or otherwise degrade or demolish something with teeth or as with teeth.
definitions.example: He keeps his feed in steel drums to prevent the mice from chewing holes in the feed-sacks.

definitions.definition: To think about something; to ponder; to chew over.
definitions.example: The professor stood at the blackboard, chalk in hand, and chewed the question the student had asked.

definitions.definition: A land area free of woodland, cities, and towns; open country.
definitions.example: There are several species of wild flowers growing in this field.

definitions.definition: A wide, open space that is usually used to grow crops or to hold farm animals.
definitions.example: A crop circle was made in a corn field.

definitions.definition: A place where competitive matches are carried out.
Any of various figurative meanings, regularly dead metaphors.
To intercept or catch (a ball) and play it.
(and other batting sports) To be the team catching and throwing the ball, as opposed to hitting it.
definitions.example: The blue team are fielding first, while the reds are batting.

definitions.definition: To place (a team, its players, etc.) in a game.
definitions.example: The away team fielded two new players and the second-choice goalkeeper.

definitions.definition: To answer; to address.
definitions.example: She will field questions immediately after her presentation.

definitions.definition: To defeat.
definitions.example: They fielded a fearsome army.

definitions.definition: To execute research (in the field).
definitions.example: He fielded the marketing survey about the upcoming product.

definitions.definition: To deploy in the field.
definitions.example: to field a new land-mine detector

definitions.definition: The act of observing; perception.
definitions.example: He took no notice of the changes, and went on as though nothing had happened.

definitions.definition: A written or printed announcement.
definitions.example: I always read the death notices in the paper.

definitions.definition: A formal notification or warning.
definitions.example: The sidewalk adjacent to the damaged bridge stonework shall be closed until further notice.

definitions.definition: Advance notification of termination of employment, given by an employer to an employee or vice versa.
definitions.example: I can't work here any longer. I'm giving notice.

definitions.definition: A published critical review of a play or the like.
Prior notification.
definitions.example: I don't mind if you want to change the venue; just give me some notice first, OK?

definitions.definition: Attention; respectful treatment; civility.
To remark upon; to mention.
To become aware of; to observe.
definitions.example: Did you notice the flowers in her yard?

definitions.definition: To lavish attention upon; to treat (someone) favourably.
To be noticeable; to show.
Emerging; just coming into existence.
Describing a quantity of object that is starting to grow from zero or an infinitesimal beginning. Also the creation or identification of an infinitesimal delta.
Describing the state, aspect, or practice of an abstract concept.

Of the state of an element at the time it is being generated from some compound or transitioning from one state to another; Newly released from a compound (especially hydrogen and oxygen) by a chemical reaction or electrolysis and possessing heightened reactivity; Newly synthesized (especially protein or RNA) by translation or transcription.
A newcomer, someone new to something.
A new user or participant; someone who is extremely new and inexperienced (to a game or activity). A beginner.
Anything recently introduced into a setting, especially something that replaces an older version.
Senses relating to a thin, pointed object.
(by extension from sense 1.1) A particular manner of expression in writing or speech, especially one regarded as good.
A particular manner of creating, doing, or presenting something, especially a work of architecture or art.
To design, fashion, make, or arrange in a certain way or form (style)
To call or give a name or title to.
To create for, or give to, someone a style, fashion, or image, particularly one which is regarded as attractive, tasteful, or trendy.
To act in a way which seeks to show that one possesses style.
A crustacean of the Nephropidae family, dark green or blue-black in colour turning bright red when cooked, with a hard shell and claws, which is used as a seafood.
A crustacean of the Palinuridae family, pinkish red in colour, with a hard, spiny shell but no claws, which is used as a seafood.
A soldier or officer of the imperial British Army (due to their red or scarlet uniform).
An Australian twenty dollar note, due to its reddish-orange colour.
To fish for lobsters.
Red-colored, especially from a sunburn.
Someone or something of low education or culture.
Unsophisticated, not intended for an audience of intelligence, education or culture.
Fine particles
The act of cleaning by dusting.
The earth, as the resting place of the dead.
The earthy remains of bodies once alive; the remains of the human body.
Something worthless.
A low or mean condition.
Cash; money (in reference to gold dust).
A disturbance or uproar.
definitions.example: to raise, or kick up, a dust

definitions.definition: A totally disconnected set of points with a fractal structure.
To remove dust from.
definitions.example: The cleaning lady needs a stool to dust the cupboard.

definitions.definition: To remove dust; to clean by removing dust.
definitions.example: Dusting always makes me cough.

definitions.definition: Of a bird, to cover itself in sand or dry, dusty earth.
To spray or cover something with fine powder or liquid.
definitions.example: The mother dusted her baby's bum with talcum powder.

definitions.definition: To leave; to rush off.
To reduce to a fine powder; to levigate.
To kill or severely disable.
A course of violent, frenzied action.
To move about wildly or violently.
The act of kowtowing.
To grovel, act in a very submissive manner.
To kneel and bow low enough to touch one’s forehead to the ground.
To bow very deeply.
A spherically contained volume of air or other gas, especially one made from soapy liquid.
A small spherical cavity in a solid material.
definitions.example: bubbles in window glass, or in a lens

definitions.definition: (by extension) Anything resembling a hollow sphere.
Anything lacking firmness or solidity; a cheat or fraud; an empty project.
A period of intense speculation in a market, causing prices to rise quickly to irrational levels as the metaphorical bubble expands, and then fall even more quickly as the bubble bursts.
definitions.example: dot-com bubble

definitions.definition: The emotional and/or physical atmosphere in which the subject is immersed.
An officer's station in a prison dormitory, affording views on all sides.
Someone who has been ‘bubbled’ or fooled; a dupe.
A small, hollow, floating bead or globe, formerly used for testing the strength of spirits.
The globule of air in the chamber of a spirit level.
A laugh.
definitions.example: Are you having a bubble?!

definitions.definition: A Greek.
Any of the small magnetized areas that make up bubble memory.

The point in a poker tournament when the last player without a prize loses all their chips and leaves the game, leaving only players that are going to win prizes. (e.g., if the last remaining 9 players win prizes, then the point when the 10th player leaves the tournament)
definitions.example: Many players tend to play timidly (not play many hands) around the bubble, to keep their chips and last longer in the game.

definitions.definition: A group of people who are in quarantine together.
To produce bubbles, to rise up in bubbles (such as in foods cooking or liquids boiling).
To churn or foment, as if wishing to rise to the surface.
definitions.example: Rage bubbled inside him.

definitions.definition: To rise through a medium or system, similar to the way that bubbles rise in liquid.
To cheat, delude.
To cry, weep.
To pat a baby on the back so as to cause it to belch.
To cause to feel as if bubbling or churning.
To express in a bubbly or lively manner.
To form into a protruding round shape.
To cover with bubbles.
To bubble in; to mark a response on a form by filling in a circular area (‘bubble’).
A cucumber preserved in a solution, usually a brine or a vinegar syrup.
definitions.example: A pickle goes well with a hamburger.

definitions.definition: (often in the plural) Any vegetable preserved in vinegar and consumed as relish.
The brine used for preserving food.
definitions.example: This tub is filled with the pickle that we will put the small cucumbers into.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation; peril.
definitions.example: The climber found himself in a pickle when one of the rocks broke off.

definitions.definition: A mildly mischievous loved one.
A rundown.
definitions.example: Jones was caught in a pickle between second and third.

definitions.definition: A children’s game with three participants that emulates a baseball rundown
definitions.example: The boys played pickle in the front yard for an hour.

definitions.definition: A penis.
A pipe for smoking methamphetamine.
definitions.example: Load some shards in that pickle.

definitions.definition: A bath of dilute sulphuric or nitric acid, etc., to remove burnt sand, scale, rust, etc., from the surface of castings, or other articles of metal, or to brighten them or improve their colour.
In an optical landing system, the hand-held controller connected to the lens, or apparatus on which the lights are mounted.
To preserve food (or sometimes other things) in a salt, sugar or vinegar solution.
definitions.example: These cucumbers pickle very well.

definitions.definition: To remove high-temperature scale and oxidation from metal with heated (often sulphuric) industrial acid.
definitions.example: The crew will pickle the fittings in the morning.

definitions.definition: (in the Python programming language) To serialize.
To pour brine over a person after flogging them, as a method of punishment.
A kernel; a grain (of salt, sugar, etc.)

A small or indefinite quantity or amount (of something); a little, a bit, a few. Usually in partitive construction, frequently without "of"; a single grain or kernel of wheat, barley, oats, sand or dust.
To eat sparingly.
To pilfer.
A stage performance, show or film that involves singing, dancing and musical numbers performed by the cast as well as acting.
A meeting or a party for a musical entertainment; a musicale.
Of, belonging or relating to music, or to its performance or notation.
definitions.example: musical proportion; musical instruments

definitions.definition: Pleasing to the ear; sounding agreeably; having the qualities of music; melodious; harmonious.
definitions.example: She had a musical voice.

definitions.definition: Fond of music; discriminating with regard to music; gifted or skilled in music.
definitions.example: the child is musical; having a musical ear

definitions.definition: Pertaining to a class of games in which players move while music plays, but have to take a fixed position when it stops; by extension, any situation where people repeatedly change positions.
A person who is egocentric.
Selfish, self-centered
Egotistical.

Relating to spatial representations: linked to a reference frame based on one's own location within the environment (as when giving the direction as "right" rather than "north"); opposed to allocentric.
Skill in the handling or manipulation of a situation.
The property of having elegance, grace, refinement, or skill.
An adroit manoeuvre.
In bridge, whist, etc.: a technique which allows one to win a trick, usually by playing a card when it is thought that a card that can beat it is held by another player whose turn is over.
To evade (a problem, situation, etc.) by using some clever argument or strategem.
To play (a card) as a finesse.
To handle or manage carefully or skilfully; to manipulate in a crafty way.
To attempt to win a trick by finessing.
To play a ball out of the way of an opponent.
New information of interest.
definitions.example: Is there any news about the storm?

definitions.definition: Information about current events disseminated via media.
definitions.example: Did you hear/read/see the latest news?

definitions.definition: Posts published on newsgroups
To report; to make known.
The large, usually yellowish-green or black, pulpy fruit of the avocado tree.
The avocado tree, Persea americana, of the laurel family.
A dark chartreuse colour, like the colour of the skin of an avocado.
Of a dull yellowish-green colour.
To make something (such as an object or message) go from one place to another.
definitions.example: Every day at two o'clock, he sends his secretary out to buy him a coffee.

definitions.definition: To excite, delight, or thrill (someone).
To bring to a certain condition.
To dispatch an agent or messenger to convey a message, or to do an errand.
definitions.example: Seeing how ill she was, we sent for a doctor at once.

definitions.definition: To cause to be or to happen; to bestow; to inflict; to grant; sometimes followed by a dependent proposition.
To pitch.
To make a successful free ascent (i.e. not relying on gear) of a sport climbing route.
definitions.example: She finally sent the 12a after hours of failed attempts.

definitions.definition: The act by which somebody or something is sent.
To engage the attention of; to awaken interest in; to excite emotion or passion in, in behalf of a person or thing.
definitions.example: Action films don't really interest me.

definitions.definition: To be concerned with or engaged in; to affect; to concern; to excite.
To cause or permit to share.
Of concern; affecting, important.
Arousing or holding the attention or interest of someone.
Pregnant.
To drive mad; to craze
Insane or mentally ill.
Suffering from dementia.
Crazy; ridiculous.
definitions.example: a demented idea

definitions.definition: An amusingly eccentric or irrational person.
Amusingly eccentric or irrational.
Hurrah!
A jointed or flexible device that allows the pivoting of a door etc.
A naturally occurring joint resembling such hardware in form or action, as in the shell of a bivalve.
A stamp hinge, a folded and gummed paper rectangle for affixing postage stamps in an album.
A principle, or a point in time, on which subsequent reasonings or events depend.
definitions.example: This argument was the hinge on which the question turned.

definitions.definition: The median of the upper or lower half of a batch, sample, or probability distribution.
One of the four cardinal points, east, west, north, or south.
To attach by, or equip with a hinge.
(with on or upon) To depend on something.

The breaking off of the distal end of a knapped stone flake whose presumed course across the face of the stone core was truncated prematurely, leaving not a feathered distal end but instead the scar of a nearly perpendicular break.
definitions.example: The flake hinged at an inclusion in the core.

definitions.definition: To bend.
A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.
The bulb of such a plant.
The genus as a whole.
A ball.
A person from Bermuda or of Bermudian descent.
To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.
definitions.example: I'm sorry. I don't understand.

definitions.definition: To believe, to think one grasps sufficiently despite potentially incomplete knowledge.
definitions.example: I understand that you have a package for me?

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside circus, acrobatics) To stand underneath, to support.

synonyms: pick up what someone is putting down

definitions.definition: Any of the plants known as mercury, especially the annual mercury or French mercury (Mercurialis annua).
A person born under the influence of the planet Mercury; hence, a person having an animated, lively, quick-witted or volatile character.
A chemical compound containing mercury.
A preparation of mercury, especially as a treatment for syphilis.
Having a lively or volatile character; animated, changeable, quick-witted.
definitions.example: his mercurial temperament

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the astrological influence of the planet Mercury; having the characteristics of a person under such influence (see adjective sense 1).
Pertaining to the planet Mercury.
Of or pertaining to the element mercury or quicksilver; containing mercury.
Caused by the action of mercury or a mercury compound.
Pertaining to Mercury, the Roman god of, among other things, commerce, financial gain, communication, and thieves and trickery; hence , money-making; crafty.
A sweet mixture of fruit boiled with sugar and allowed to congeal. Often spread on bread or toast or used in jam tarts.
A difficult situation.
Blockage, congestion.
definitions.example: A traffic jam caused us to miss the game's first period.

definitions.definition: An informal, impromptu performance or rehearsal.
(by extension) A song; a track.
(by extension) An informal event where people brainstorm and collaborate on projects.
To get something stuck in a confined space.
definitions.example: Her poor little baby toe got jammed in the door.

definitions.definition: To brusquely force something into a space; cram, squeeze.
definitions.example: The rush-hour train was jammed with commuters.

definitions.definition: To cause congestion or blockage. Often used with "up"
definitions.example: A single accident can jam the roads for hours.

definitions.definition: To block or confuse a broadcast signal.
To throw a pitch at or near the batter's hands.
definitions.example: Jones was jammed by the pitch.

definitions.definition: To play music (especially improvisation as a group, or an informal unrehearsed session).
To injure a finger or toe by sudden compression of the digit's tip.
definitions.example: When he tripped on the step he jammed his toe.

definitions.definition: To attempt to score points.
definitions.example: Toughie jammed four times in the second period.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.
To give up on a date or some joint endeavour; stand up, chicken out, jam out.
A kind of frock for children.
(interior decorating) Either of the vertical components that form the side of an opening in a wall, such as that of a door frame, window frame, or fireplace.
Any thick mass of rock that prevents miners from following the lode or vein.
A literal or metaphorical force or object regarded as unstoppable, that will crush all in its path.
(sometimes derogatory) A large, cumbersome truck or lorry, especially an artic.
An institution that incites destructive devotion or to which people are carelessly sacrificed.
A small explosive device, designed to be thrown by hand or launched from a grenade launcher.
A pomegranate.
A charge similar to a fireball, and made of a disc-shaped bomb shell, but with only one set of flames at the top.
An unattractive girl.
To use grenade(s) upon.

A grating; a grid of wire or a sheet of material with a pattern of holes or slots, usually used to protect something while allowing the passage of air and liquids. Typical uses: to allow air through a fan while preventing fingers or objects from passing; to allow people to talk to somebody, while preventing attack.
On a vehicle, a slotted cover as above, to protect and hide the radiator, while admitting air to cool it.
A cooking device comprising a source of radiative heat and a means of holding food under it; a broiler in US English
A cooking device comprising a source of radiative and convective heat and a means of holding food above it; a barbecue.
definitions.example: I put some peppers and mushrooms on the grill to go with dinner.

definitions.definition: Food (designed to be) cooked on a grill.
definitions.example: a packet of frozen cauliflower cheese grills

definitions.definition: A grillroom; a restaurant serving grilled food.
definitions.example: These coupons will get you a discount at Johnny's Bar and Grill.

definitions.definition: A type of jewelry worn on the front teeth.
(by extension) The front teeth regarded collectively.
To cook (food) on a grill; to barbecue.
definitions.example: Why don't we get together Saturday and grill some burgers?

definitions.definition: To cook food under the element of a stove or only under the top element of an oven – broil, salamander.
To interrogate; to question aggressively or harshly.
definitions.example: The police grilled him about his movements at the time of the crime.

definitions.definition: To feel very hot; to swelter.
To stamp or mark with a grill.
In a generous manner, in a way that is giving or ample.
definitions.example: He generously donated what little he could.

definitions.definition: A mother of someone's parent.
A female ancestor or progenitor.
A girl or an unmarried young woman; maiden.
A female servant or cleaner (short for maidservant).
A virgin, now female but originally one of either gender.
An effeminate man, especially a homosexual.
A woman or girl employed to care for children
Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.
definitions.example: He knew that it was trash day, when the garbage collectors made all the noise.

definitions.definition: Sound or signal generated by random fluctuations.
Unwanted part of a signal.
(by extension) Unwanted fuss or bustle; useless activity.
The measured level of variation in gene expression among cells, regardless of source, within a supposedly identical population.
Rumour or complaint.
definitions.example: The problems with the new computer system are causing a lot of noise at Head Office.

definitions.definition: Music, in general; a concert; also, a company of musicians; a band.
A genre of rock music that uses static and other non-musical sounds, also influenced by art rock.
To make a noise; to sound.
To spread news of; to spread as rumor or gossip.
Brushing or flickering gently over a surface.
Glowing or luminous, but lacking heat.
definitions.example: The lambent glow of fireflies delighted the children.

definitions.definition: Exhibiting lightness or brilliance of wit; clever or witty without unkindness.
definitions.example: We appreciated her lambent comments.

definitions.definition: A device on or adjacent to an outer door for announcing one's presence. It can be mechanical, directly sounding a bell, or a button that electrically sounds a chime or buzzer inside the building.
A button that actives an electric doorbell.
To ring many doorbells in an effort to contact people and thereby spread information or solicit.
To ring many doorbells of (target people or an area) in an effort to contact people and thereby spread information or solicit.
definitions.example: We doorbelled the whole district in an effort to get out the vote.

definitions.definition: Someone who or something that trails.
Part of an object which extends some distance beyond the main body of the object.
definitions.example: the trailer of a plant

definitions.definition: An unpowered wheeled vehicle, not a caravan or camper, that is towed behind another, and used to carry equipment, etc, that cannot be carried in the leading vehicle.
definitions.example: At the end of the day, we put the snowmobiles back on the trailer.

definitions.definition: A furnished vehicle towed behind another, and used as a dwelling when stationary; a caravan; a camper.
definitions.example: We drove our trailer to Yellowstone Park.

definitions.definition: A prefabricated home that could be towed to a new destination but is typically permanently left in an area designated for such homes.
definitions.example: The young couple′s first home was in a trailer.

definitions.definition: A preview of a film, video game or TV show.
definitions.example: The trailer for that movie makes it seem like it would be fun.

definitions.definition: A short blank segment of film at the end of a reel, for convenient insertion of the film in a projector.

The final record of a list of data items, often identified by a key field with an otherwise invalid value that sorts last alphabetically (e.g., “ZZZZZ”) or numerically (“99999”); especially common in the context of punched cards, where the final card is called a trailer card.
definitions.example: The linked list terminates with a trailer record.

definitions.definition: The last part of a packet, often containing a check sequence.
definitions.example: The encapsulation layer adds an eight-byte header and a two-byte trailer to each packet.

definitions.definition: To load on a trailer or to transport by trailer.
definitions.example: The engine wouldn't run any more so we had to trailer my old car to the wrecking yard.

definitions.definition: One who has won or often wins.
A point or goal that wins a competition.
A device designed to be placed in the mouth and blown, or driven by steam or some other mechanism, to make a whistling sound.
An act of whistling.
A shrill, high-pitched sound made by whistling.
Any high-pitched sound similar to the sound made by whistling.
definitions.example: the whistle of the wind in the trees

definitions.definition: A suit (from whistle and flute).
The mouth and throat; so called as being the organs of whistling.
To make a shrill, high-pitched sound by forcing air through the mouth. To produce a whistling sound, restrictions to the flow of air are created using the teeth, tongue and lips.
definitions.example: Never whistle at a funeral.

definitions.definition: To make a similar sound by forcing air through a musical instrument or a pipe etc.
definitions.example: The stream train whistled as it passed by.

definitions.definition: To move in such a way as to create a whistling sound.
definitions.example: A bullet whistled past.

definitions.definition: To send, signal, or call by a whistle.
Something that is owned.
definitions.example: Important types of property include real property (land), personal property (other physical possessions), and intellectual property (rights over artistic creations, inventions, etc.).

definitions.definition: A piece of real estate, such as a parcel of land.
definitions.example: There is a large house on the property.

definitions.definition: Real estate; the business of selling houses.
definitions.example: He works in property as a housing consultant.

definitions.definition: The exclusive right of possessing, enjoying and disposing of a thing.
An attribute or abstract quality associated with an individual, object or concept.
definitions.example: Charm is his most endearing property.

definitions.definition: An attribute or abstract quality which is characteristic of a class of objects.
definitions.example: Matter can have many properties, including color, mass and density.

definitions.definition: An editable or read-only parameter associated with an application, component or class, or the value of such a parameter.
definitions.example: You need to set the debugging property to "verbose".

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A prop, an object used in a dramatic production.
definitions.example: Costumes and scenery are distinguished from property properly speaking.

definitions.definition: Propriety; correctness.
To invest with properties, or qualities.
To make a property of; to appropriate.
Distant.
definitions.example: She lived in a faraway village in a faraway land.

definitions.definition: Not mentally present, as when daydreaming.
definitions.example: There was a faraway look on his face.

definitions.definition: To become conscious after having slept.
To cause (somebody) to stop sleeping.
To excite or to stir up something latent.
To rouse from a state of inaction or dormancy.
To come out of a state of inaction or dormancy.
The act of awaking, or ceasing to sleep.
A revival of religion, or more general attention to religious matters than usual.
Being roused into action or activity.
One who procrastinates or is lazy.
A person lacking a sense of direction in life; an underachiever.
A person who seeks to avoid military service.
A user of the Slackware Linux operating system.
A member of a certain 1990s subculture associated with Generation X.
The name of the Latin-script letter I.
definitions.example: the position of an i-dot (the dot of an i)

definitions.definition: A statement of the meaning of a word or word group or a sign or symbol (dictionary definitions).
(usually with the definite article the) A clear instance conforming to the dictionary or textbook definition.
A statement expressing the essential nature of something; formulation
The action or process of defining.
The act of defining; determination of the limits.
A product of defining.
The action or power of describing, explaining, or making definite and clear.
definitions.example: Her comic genius is beyond definition.

definitions.definition: Clarity of visual presentation, distinctness of outline or detail.
definitions.example: Improve the definition of an image.

definitions.definition: Clarity, especially of musical sound in reproduction.
Sharp demarcation of outlines or limits.
definitions.example: A jacket with distinct waist definition.

definitions.definition: The degree to which individual muscles are distinct on the body.
A statement which provides a previous declaration with a value or body of a subroutine (in the case of function).
A statement that establishes the referent of a term or notation.
A wild flowering plant Bellis perennis of the Asteraceae family, with a yellow head and white petals
Many other flowering plants of various species.
A boot or other footwear.
To bring in a harvest; reap; glean.
To be occupied bringing in a harvest
definitions.example: Harvesting is a stressing, thirsty occupation

definitions.definition: To win, achieve a gain.
definitions.example: The rising star harvested well-deserved acclaim, even an Oscar under 21

definitions.definition: Deserving of ridicule; foolish; absurd.
definitions.example: It's ridiculous to charge so much for a little souvenir.

definitions.definition: Astonishing; unbelievable.
definitions.example: Gaines is a classic motor player with a low center of gravity and ridiculous strength.

definitions.definition: A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.
A minor nobleman with an honourable military rank who had served as a page and squire.
(by extension) An armored and mounted warrior of the Middle Ages.
definitions.example: King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table

definitions.definition: (modern) A person on whom a knighthood has been conferred by a monarch.
A brave, chivalrous and honorable man devoted to a noble cause or love interest.
A chess piece, often in the shape of a horse's head, that is moved two squares in one direction and one at right angles to that direction in a single move, leaping over any intervening pieces.
A playing card bearing the figure of a knight; the knave or jack.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Ypthima.
(modern) A generic name for various mushrooms belonging to the fungal order gilled mushrooms; scientific name Tricholoma.
To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.
definitions.example: Did you kick your brother?

definitions.definition: To make a sharp jerking movement of the leg, as to strike something.
definitions.example: He enjoyed the simple pleasure of watching the kickline kick.

definitions.definition: To direct to a particular place by a blow with the foot or leg.
definitions.example: Kick the ball into the goal.

definitions.definition: (with "off" or "out") To eject summarily.
To forcibly remove a participant from an online activity.
definitions.example: He was kicked by ChanServ for flooding.

definitions.definition: To overcome (a bothersome or difficult issue or obstacle); to free oneself of (a problem).
definitions.example: I still smoke, but they keep telling me to kick the habit.

definitions.definition: To move or push suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: He was kicked sideways by the force of the blast.

definitions.definition: (of a firearm) To recoil; to push by recoiling.
To attack (a piece) in order to force it to move.
To accelerate quickly with a few pedal strokes in an effort to break away from other riders.
definitions.example: Contador kicks again to try to rid himself of Rasmussen.

definitions.definition: To show opposition or resistance.
To work a press by impact of the foot on a treadle.
To die.
(smoking, of a pipe) Empty with nothing left to smoke but ash.
Fake; not real
Very impressive; amazing; unbelievable; incredible; larger or more fantastic than typical of real life.
definitions.example: I just had an unreal hamburger.

definitions.definition: Willingness of action especially in behavior.
The action of motivating.
Something which motivates.
An incentive or reason for doing something.
A research rating that measures how the rational and emotional elements of a commercial affect consumer intention to consider, visit, or buy something.
definitions.example: The motivation scores showed that 65% of people wanted to visit our website to learn more about the offer after watching the commercial.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.
definitions.example: I didn’t mean what I said — I was only joking.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.
definitions.example: Relax, man, I'm just joking with you.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.
definitions.example: to joke a comrade

definitions.definition: The condition of being friends.
A friendly relationship, or a relationship as friends.
Good will.
One who, or that which, slaps.
A prostitute.
A woman of loose morals.
Anything monstrous; a whopper.
To connect two or more things.
(of a Web page) To contain a hyperlink to another page.
definitions.example: My homepage links to my wife's.

definitions.definition: To supply (somebody) with a hyperlink; to direct by means of a link.
definitions.example: Haven't you seen his Web site? I'll link you to it.

definitions.definition: To post a hyperlink to.
definitions.example: Stop linking those unfunny comics all the time!

definitions.definition: To demonstrate a correlation between two things.
To combine objects generated by a compiler into a single executable.
To skip or trip along smartly; to go quickly.
Linkage
Serving to connect other things together.
definitions.example: Conjunctions frequently function as linking words in a sentence.

definitions.definition: One who investigates.
A toy in the form of a human.
(sometimes offensive) An attractive young woman
A term of endearment: darling, sweetheart.
A dollar.
(now possibly offensive) A good-natured, cooperative or helpful girl.
The smallest or pet pig in a litter.
The suit, in a game of cards, that outranks all others.
definitions.example: Diamonds were declared trump(s).

definitions.definition: A playing card of that suit.
definitions.example: He played an even higher trump.

definitions.definition: Something that gives one an advantage, especially one held in reserve.
An excellent person; a fine fellow, a good egg.
An old card game, almost identical to whist; the game of ruff.
A card of the major arcana of the tarot.
To play on (a card of another suit) with a trump.
definitions.example: He knew the hand was lost when his ace was trumped.

definitions.definition: To play a trump, or to take a trick with a trump.
To get the better of, or finesse, a competitor.
To impose unfairly; to palm off.
To supersede.
definitions.example: In this election, it would seem issues of national security trumped economic issues.

definitions.definition: To outweigh; be stronger, greater, bigger than or in other way superior to.
The board one stands on for wakeboarding.
To take part in wakeboarding.
The place where someone works.
The distance from the base of something to the top.
The vertical distance from the ground to the highest part of a standing person or animal (withers in the case of a horse).
The highest point or maximum degree.
definitions.example: She's at the height of her career.

definitions.definition: A high point.
An area of land at the top of a cliff.
The act of killing.
definitions.example: The assassin liked to make a clean kill, and thus favored small arms over explosives.

definitions.definition: Specifically, the death blow.
definitions.example: The hunter delivered the kill with a pistol shot to the head.

definitions.definition: The result of killing; that which has been killed.
definitions.example: The fox dragged its kill back to its den.

definitions.definition: The grounding of the ball on the opponent's court, winning the rally.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
Such a file transfer.
To transfer data to a computer on a network, especially to a server on the Internet.
definitions.example: You will have to convert your photograph to a jpg format if you wish to upload it.

definitions.definition: A long, broad and thick piece of timber, as opposed to a board which is less thick.
A political issue that is of concern to a faction or a party of the people and the political position that is taken on that issue.

definitions.definition: Physical exercise in which one holds a pushup position for a measured length of time.
A stupid person, idiot.
That which supports or upholds.
To cover something with planking.

definitions.definition: To bake (fish, etc.) on a piece of cedar lumber.
To lay down, as on a plank or table; to stake or pay cash.

definitions.definition: To harden, as hat bodies, by felting.
To splice together the ends of slivers of wool, for subsequent drawing.
To pose for a photograph while lying rigid, face down, arms at side, in an unusual place.
A book in which notes or memoranda are written.
Short for notebook computer.
Short for awesomeness: the quality, state, or essence of being awesome.
definitions.example: pure awesome; made of awesome

definitions.definition: Causing awe or terror; inspiring wonder or excitement.
definitions.example: The tsunami was awesome in its destructive power.

definitions.definition: Excellent, exciting, remarkable.
definitions.example: Awesome, dude!

definitions.definition: Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.
A luminous celestial body, made up of plasma (particularly hydrogen and helium) and having a spherical shape. Depending on context the sun may or may not be included.
A concave polygon with regular, pointy protrusions and indentations, generally with five or six points.
An actor in a leading role.
definitions.example: Many Hollywood stars attended the launch party.

definitions.definition: An exceptionally talented or famous person, often in a specific field; a celebrity.
definitions.example: His teacher tells us he is a star pupil.

definitions.definition: An asterisk (*).
A symbol used to rate hotels, films, etc. with a higher number of stars denoting better quality.

A simple dance, or part of a dance, where a group of four dancers each put their right or left hand in the middle and turn around in a circle. You call them right-hand stars or left-hand stars, depending on the hand which is in the middle.
A planet supposed to influence one's destiny.
definitions.example: What's in the stars for you today? Find out in our horoscope.

definitions.definition: A star-shaped ornament worn on the breast to indicate rank or honour.
A composition of combustible matter used in the heading of rockets, in mines, etc., which, exploding in the air, presents a starlike appearance.
To appear as a featured performer or headliner, especially in an entertainment program.
To feature (a performer or a headliner), especially in a movie or an entertainment program.
To mark with a star or asterisk.
To set or adorn with stars, or bright, radiating bodies; to bespangle.
To shine like a star.
(with "the") Outer space.
A venomous snake in the family Viperidae.
A dangerous, treacherous, or malignant person.
A person who smokes marijuana.
Something that happens to someone by chance, a chance occurrence, especially a favourable one.
definitions.example: Gilbert had some bad luck yesterday — he got pick-pocketed and lost fifty dollars.

definitions.definition: A superstitious feeling that brings fortune or success.
definitions.example: He blew on the dice for luck.

definitions.definition: Success.
definitions.example: He has a lot of luck with the ladies, perhaps it is because of his new motorbike.

definitions.definition: The results of a random number generator.
definitions.example: The creators of tool-assisted speedruns often manipulate a lot of luck to get the most favorable results in order to save the most time.

definitions.definition: To succeed by chance.
definitions.example: His plan lucked out.

definitions.definition: To rely on luck.
definitions.example: No plan. We're just to going to have to luck through.

definitions.definition: To carry out relying on luck.
definitions.example: Our plan is to luck it through.

definitions.definition: To roll or fold up; to wind round; to entwine.
To envelop completely; to surround; to cover; to hide.

definitions.definition: To complicate or make intricate, as in grammatical structure.
To connect with something as a natural or logical consequence or effect; to include necessarily; to imply.
To take in; to gather in; to mingle confusedly; to blend or merge.
To envelop, enfold, entangle.

definitions.definition: To engage (someone) to participate in a task.

definitions.definition: To raise to any assigned power; to multiply, as a quantity, into itself a given number of times.

definitions.definition: To damage beyond use or repair.

definitions.definition: To neutralize, undo a property or condition.

definitions.definition: To put down or euthanize.

definitions.definition: To severely disrupt the well-being of (a person); ruin.
definitions.example: Her divorce destroyed her; she had a nervous breakdown and was severely depressed for more than a year.

definitions.definition: To defeat soundly.
To remove data.
definitions.example: The memory leak happened because we forgot to destroy the temporary lists.

definitions.definition: To sing a song poorly.
(antiphrasis) To exhaust duly and thus recreate or build up.

definitions.definition: To penetrate sexually in an aggressive way.

definitions.definition: To jump better than; particularly higher than, or further than.
A destructive capacity that exceeds that needed to destroy an enemy; especially with nuclear weapons.

definitions.definition: An unnecessary excess of disposal because of too high criteria of inspection.
To destroy something with more (nuclear) force than is required.
To dispose of too many items because of too high criteria of inspection.
To do something excessive to achieve a goal.
The currency of Iceland, Denmark (including Greenland and the Faroe Islands) and Norway, divided into 100 øre, except in Iceland where 1 króna = 100 aurar.
To know, perceive or understand.
To discover by sight; to catch sight of; to descry.
The brother or brother-in-law of one’s parent.
The male cousin of one’s parent.
A companion to one's (usually unmarried) mother.
A source of advice, encouragement, or help.
A pawnbroker.
An affectionate term for a man of an older generation than oneself, especially a friend of one's parents, by means of fictive kin.
An older male African-American person.
To address somebody by the term uncle.
To act like, or as, an uncle.
A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.

definitions.definition: Such hardened mud, clay, etc. considered collectively, as a building material.

definitions.definition: Something shaped like a brick.

definitions.definition: A helpful and reliable person.

definitions.definition: A shot which misses, particularly one which bounces directly out of the basket because of a too-flat trajectory, as if the ball were a heavier object.

An electronic device, especially a heavy box-shaped one, that has become non-functional or obsolete.
A carton of 500 rimfire cartridges, which forms the approximate size and shape of a brick.
A community card (usually the turn or the river) which does not improve a player's hand.

definitions.definition: The colour brick red.
One kilo of cocaine.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.

definitions.definition: (of weather) Extremely cold.

definitions.definition: A small body of standing or stagnant water; a puddle.
A supply of resources.

A small amount of liquid on a surface.

definitions.definition: A localized glow of light.

The human race in its entirety.
Men collectively, as opposed to all women.
Human feelings; humanity.
A migratory route used by birds between breeding areas.
The act of neglecting.
The state of being neglected.
Habitual lack of care.
To fail to care for or attend to something.

definitions.definition: To omit to notice; to forbear to treat with attention or respect; to slight.

The insertion of needles into the (living) tissue of the body, especially for purposes of pain relief.
To treat with acupuncture.
In a swift manner; quickly; with quick motion or velocity; fleetly.
A chemical or compound that changes into a gas easily.
Evaporating or vaporizing readily under normal conditions.

Fickle.
Temporary or ephemeral.

The story or truth.

definitions.definition: Unfair; shameful.

To die from suffocation while immersed in water or other fluid.

definitions.definition: To write a critical evaluation of a new art work etc.; to write a review.
definitions.example: The critic reviews every new play in London.

definitions.definition: To look back over in order to correct or edit; to revise.

To view or see again; to look back on.
To retrace; to go over again.
The act of killing.

definitions.definition: Specifically, the death blow.

definitions.example: The fox dragged its kill back to its den.

definitions.definition: The grounding of the ball on the opponent's court, winning the rally.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
(north-east US) A creek; a body of water; a channel or arm of the sea.
definitions.example: Schuylkill, Catskill, etc.

definitions.definition: A kiln.
A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.
Being the only one of its kind; unequaled, unparalleled or unmatched.
definitions.example: Every person has a unique life, therefore every person has a unique journey. ― Gary Cook

definitions.definition: Of a feature, such that only one holder has it.
Particular, characteristic.
Of a rare quality, unusual.
An arid terrain characterized by severe erosion of sedimentary rocks.
A community of bohemians, unconventional artists or writers.
